subjects.description: the AWS account ID owning the instance
the AMI (image) ID the instance was launched from
the EC2 instance ID from the instance identity document
the private IPv4 address of the instance
the CodeBuild build ID (CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID)
the CodeBuild project name (CODEBUILD_PROJECT_NAME)
the resolved source commit (CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION)

upstream.name: GCP Metadata Server / Instance Identity Token

subjects.description: GKE cluster UID (populated for workload-identity / GKE nodes)
GCP instance ID (numeric)
GCP instance hostname / name
GCP project ID
GCP numeric project number
the commit author email
the HEAD commit SHA-1
the commit committer email
each parent commit SHA-1 of HEAD
the short name of the checked-out ref (branch)
each configured remote URL (credentials stripped)
the GitHub Actions workflow run URL (server/repo/actions/runs/<run-id>)
the GitHub repository URL (server/owner/repo)
the GitLab CI job URL (CI_JOB_URL)
the GitLab CI pipeline URL (CI_PIPELINE_URL)
the GitLab CI project URL (CI_PROJECT_URL)
the JENKINS_URL controller base URL (sha256 of the URL string)
the BUILD_URL of the Jenkins pipeline run (sha256 of the URL string)

each discovered lockfile, keyed file:<path> (e.g. Gemfile.lock, package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Cargo.lock, poetry.lock, Pipfile.lock, requirements.txt, composer.lock, go.sum, Podfile.lock, gradle.lockfile, pnpm-lock.yaml); path is relative to the search root and digested with SHA256

applies_when.pre: any_of(file_exists:Cargo.lock, file_exists:Gemfile.lock, file_exists:Pipfile.lock, file_exists:composer.lock, file_exists:go.sum, file_exists:package-lock.json, file_exists:pnpm-lock.yaml, file_exists:poetry.lock, file_exists:requirements.txt, file_exists:yarn.lock)

subjects.description: each declared Maven dependency, formatted dependency:<groupId>/<artifactId>@<version>
the Maven project itself, formatted project:<groupId>/<artifactId>@<version> from pom.xml coordinates
the scanned AWS account id (from the first finding carrying one)
each unique resource ARN referenced by a FAILED finding
ARN of each CRITICAL or HIGH severity FAILED finding
each evaluated resource as <resourceType>/<resourceId>
each unique AWS Config rule name from the evaluation results
AWS account id parsed from any ResourceId that is an ARN (field 5)
sha256 image digest (sha256: prefix trimmed) parsed from containerimage.digest
image.name reference string from the buildx metadata
sha256 digest of a SLSA provenance build material
URI of a SLSA provenance build material

applies_when.pre: any_of(argv_prefix:[buildah build], argv_prefix:[docker build], argv_prefix:[docker buildx build])
applies_when.post: any_of(exec_observed(argv_prefix:[buildah build]), exec_observed(argv_prefix:[docker build]), exec_observed(argv_prefix:[docker buildx build]))

subjects.description: the CIS Docker Benchmark identity (e.g. benchmark:cis-docker@1.3.4), derived from the tool's reported version
each running container name docker-bench audited in its Container Runtime section (tests id "5")
the Kubernetes cluster name from $FALCO_CLUSTER_NAME; emitted only when set
the host whose Falco runtime events were captured (os.Hostname of the cilock node); emitted only when hostname is non-empty
the captured Falco event file, keyed by its real product-set digest so the BFS can chain back to product/v0.3; emitted only when the report digest set is non-empty
one subject per distinct Falco rule that fired in the captured window
each scan-root Go module path (summary.scanRoots, from the SBOM message roots)
each unique OSV id whose finding is call-graph reachable (symbol-level trace)
one subject per control that had at least one failing result, keyed by control id
scanned platform as <os-name> or <os-name>-<release> (single subject)
one subject per InSpec profile, keyed by profile name
CIS Kubernetes Benchmark identity (e.g. benchmark:cis-kubernetes or benchmark:cis-kubernetes-<version>); always emitted
target cluster name from KUBE_BENCH_CLUSTER_NAME; emitted only when set
hostname of the node that was scanned (os.Hostname); emitted only when non-empty
path of the linkerd check -o json report file in the product set (digested by the product set)
cluster name from $LINKERD_CLUSTER_NAME (only emitted when the env var is set)
path of the optional linkerd viz edges -o json report file (only emitted when an edges file is present)
top-level pass/fail signal, suffix is 'success' or 'fail' based on CheckSummary.OverallSuccess
sha256 of the image config blob (the OCI image ID)
one subject per RepoTag from manifest.json (key tail is the literal image tag)
per-layer uncompressed diffID; key is layerdiffid<NN>: where NN is the zero-padded layer index
sha256 of the raw manifest.json bytes extracted from the tar
digest of the docker-save tar product file

applies_when.post: any_of(exec_observed(argv_prefix:[crane]), exec_observed(argv_prefix:[docker save]), exec_observed(argv_prefix:[skopeo copy]), product_glob:[*.oci.tar, **/*.oci.tar, oci-image.tar, **/oci-image.tar], product_glob:[index.json, **/index.json, oci-layout, **/oci-layout])

subjects.description: the XCCDF benchmark document id (Benchmark/@id)
the target system that was scanned (TestResult/target)
the XCCDF profile id that was evaluated (TestResult/profile/@idref)
One subject per installed package, keyed pip://<name>@<version>; the digest is sha256 of the literal "<name>==<version>" string, not the wheel artifact.
the AWS account id scanned (Summary.AccountId)
each unique resource ARN from a failed check (FailedCheck.ResourceArn)
each unique AWS service name from a failed check (FailedCheck.ServiceName)
the SBOM file path (from the product set, or the --attestor-sbom-file path)
the document/component name (SPDX documentName or CycloneDX metadata.component.name)
the CycloneDX metadata.component.version, when present
pip://NAME@VERSION, byte-identical to the pip-install attestor's subject (digest = sha256 of "NAME==VERSION"); emitted only when PIPW_PACKAGE_NAME and PIPW_PACKAGE_VERSION are both set
pipw-sinkhole-flows-file://SCAN_ID with digest = sha256 of the captured /flows/out.jsonl file; emitted only when the flows file was present and hashed
pipw-sinkhole-scan://SCAN_ID (digest = sha256 of the scan id); emitted only when PIPW_SCAN_ID is set
AWS account_id column value (aws plugin)
AWS resource arn column value (aws plugin)
AWS region column value (aws plugin)
GitHub org login (github plugin)
GitHub name_with_owner repo slug (github plugin)
Kubernetes namespace column value (kubernetes plugin)
Kubernetes resource uid column value (kubernetes plugin)
Okta organization column value (okta plugin)
Okta user id column value (okta plugin)
SHA-256 of each failed test's fully-qualified name (classname.name, suite::name, or bare name).
SHA-256 of each distinct top-level test-suite name observed in the report.

applies_when.pre: any_of(argv_prefix:[mal analyze], argv_prefix:[mal diff], argv_prefix:[mal scan])
any_of(argv_prefix:[npm ci], argv_prefix:[npm i], argv_prefix:[npm install])

parse_errors.github-review: detector.yaml category "statement" must be one of [source-checkout ci-context dependency-resolve dependency-verify build unit-test integration-test code-review threat-model vulnerability-scan secret-scan compliance-scan sbom-generate sbom-consume provenance policy-eval sign publish deploy lint release-approve archive iac-plan iac-apply manifest-validate image-build image-scan image-sign package-publish runtime-event runtime-vulnerability-detect drift-detect asset-inventory vex-consume vulnerability-disclosure incident-response rollback key-ceremony api-surface-check model-train model-eval dataset-snapshot firmware-sign mobile-sign mobile-submit]
